title: molinia remota
intro: Molinia remota, also known as purple moor-grass, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as wound healing, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in folk remedies to aid in the relief of fever, cough, and sore throats.
